frame_analyses.description: Instruments positioned at rest; left needle driver (black handle) and right needle driver (silver handle) visible; Penrose drain in target position with two marked entry points visible on foam structure
Black needle driver approaching needle; silver driver retracted; needle visible with suture attached
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth approach; needle position appears ready for capture

frame_analyses.description: Black driver holding needle; silver driver assisting with suture control; needle beginning approach to first mark on Penrose drain
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good needle grasp; two-handed coordination evident

frame_analyses.description: Needle penetrating foam at first mark; entry appears close to marked location; suture trailing from puncture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry angle appears perpendicular; placement near first mark

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting at second mark; white suture now spanning the two entry/exit points on drain; both marks appear engaged by suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement complete; linear path between marks

frame_analyses.description: Suture now visible outside trainer box area; black driver holding suture end; silver driver positioning for first throw (surgeon's knot double throw); preparing loop formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Extracorporeal knot formation initiated; suture ends separated

frame_analyses.description: First throw (double loop) forming between drivers; black driver wrapping suture around silver driver; visible loop structure with two wraps
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw appearance; good hand position and coordination

frame_analyses.description: First throw tightening; knot cinching between drivers; loop compressing as drivers work in opposition
First throw completed and seated; second throw (single) now forming with silver driver leading this throw; loop visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch evident; silver driver now leading; alternating throw pattern observed

frame_analyses.description: Second throw tightening; knot structure now shows two complete throws stacked; both appear secure
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Alternating hand leadership maintained; knot progressing well

frame_analyses.description: Completed knot held between drivers; preparing to advance knot down suture toward drain; knot appears tight and well-formed
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot quality appears good; ready for pusher insertion

frame_analyses.description: Knot being advanced down suture toward drain using pusher technique; knot moving along suture shaft; approaching drain entry point
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth advancement; knot maintaining integrity as it travels

frame_analyses.description: Knot now seated at or very near the drain entry points; suture tails visible extending from knot; scissors visible at edge of frame in preparation for cutting
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot positioning appears correct at entry point

frame_analyses.description: Suture being cut; scissors engaged with one suture end; first tail being severed close to knot
Second suture tail being trimmed; both ends now cut short; knot secured at drain with minimal tail length remaining
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Neat completion; both suture ends trimmed

penalties.description: Entry point at first mark appears approximately 1mm lateral to marked center
Exit point at second mark appears approximately 1mm lateral to marked center

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw with two visible wraps; good tension control; knot sits flat and compact
Hand switch from throw 1 to throw 2 executed correctly; single throw clean and secure
Hand switch back to black driver; third throw tied; all throws alternate hands as required

suture_placement.notes: Both entry/exit points very close to marked locations; minor lateral deviation estimated from frame 4-5 viewing angle

confidence_rationale: Video provides clear visualization of all phases from needle load through suture cut. Completion time is precisely measurable. Knot formation and hand switching are clearly observable and executed correctly. Suture deviation at marks is estimated from angle-dependent frames with ~0.65 confidence; actual deviation likely in 0.5–1.5mm range per mark. Drain remains intact throughout; knot quality excellent. Overall scoring highly reliable except for precise mm measurements of mark deviation.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation from mark 1 — camera view slightly oblique to entry point
Exact millimeter deviation from mark 2 — camera view slightly oblique to exit point
Knot pusher brand/type — tool visible but not fully identifiable
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance. All five required phases executed in sequence with minimal time loss. Suture placement accurate and near marks. Knot formation textbook-quality with proper surgeon's knot double throw followed by two alternating single throws. Hand switching observed and correct throughout. Knot advancement smooth and secure. Drain integrity maintained; no gaps or avulsion. Final result shows clean, tight knot at drain entry with minimal tail length.
strengths: Rapid needle load and suture placement — well under 15s combined
Exemplary knot formation with visible double throw and alternating hand switches
Smooth extracorporeal knot advancement with no knot slippage or breakage
Efficient suture trimming with clean cuts and minimal cleanup time
improvement_suggestions: Aim for zero deviation from marks — current ~1mm per mark is acceptable but can be tightened with perpendicular entry practice
All other phases are expert-level; no significant improvements needed
